channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Carlyle Rethinks Portfolio Risk to Give Weather Insurance a Bigger Role
item.description: is unveiling a new framework for portfolio risk so that asset values reflect the insurance implications associated with severe weather shocks. “We want to flip the paradigm and de-risk it so that insurance continues to be available” to assets that are in the crosshairs, Steve Hatfield, co-head of global sustainability at Carlyle, said in an interview. But so far, there’s been “no consistent mechanism that allows insurers to recognize how asset hardening reduces risk.” Asset hardening in this context refers to the process of making a portfolio’s contents more resistant to the fallout from rising temperatures.

item.title: 13 Dead, Dozens Injured, After Blast During Restart at Giant Qatar LNG Site
item.description: Thirteen people were killed and dozens injured after an explosion at Qatar’s massive Ras Laffan liquefied natural (LNG) gas complex which occurred as workers were restarting operations halted after an Iranian attack in March. Speaking to reporters on Monday, Qatar’s Energy Ministry Saad al-Kaabi said 13 people had died and 66 were injured. military base, has come under repeated Iranian missile and drone attacks during the Iran war, which trapped around 20% of global LNG supply in the Gulf before some shipments began to resume recently.

item.title: Horse-Drawn Carriage Rides On Hold After Teen Tourist’s Death in New York
item.description: Horse carriage rides in Central Park are on hold at least until Tuesday, the union representing drivers said, as safety protocols are reviewed in the wake of the death of a young tourist who jumped from a runaway horse carriage. Alexander Kemp, administrative vice president of Transport Workers Union Local 100, said in a statement Friday that “our hearts go out to the family” of Romanch Mahajan, 18, who died Wednesday after he hit his head while jumping off the four-wheeled carriage as its horse sprinted through the park without the driver. Kemp said at a news conference Friday that the union is creating new safety training protocols with feedback from horse carriage operators to be rolled out next week and that about 200 carriage owners and drivers will be required to follow.

item.title: Waymo Recalls Nearly 3,900 Robotaxis Over Risk of Entering Closed Construction Zones
item.description: Waymo updated vehicle software to avoid entering construction zones. Alphabet’s self-driving unit, Waymo, is recalling nearly 3,900 robotaxis in the U.S. The latest recall, filed with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), stems from more than a dozen incidents since early April in California and Arizona in which Waymo autonomous vehicles (AVs) did not recognize and drove past ramp closure signs into pre-planned freeway construction zones and freeway lanes with active construction work.

item.title: California UPS Mechanic Admits to Workers’ Comp Fraud
item.description: A former UPS employee was sentenced to 90 days in jail, two years’ probation, and ordered to pay $50,000 restitution to Liberty Mutual Insurance after submitting a fraudulent workers’ compensation claim, according to a press release issued last week by the Sacramento County District Attorney’s Office. The former mechanic at the United Parcel Service (UPS) claimed that on March 3, 2023, he was injured while walking out of a door at the UPS facility when a “microwave-sized” chunk of ice fell from the roof and struck him on the back of his head and neck. In 2025, the Sacramento County District Attorney’s Insurance Fraud Unit began an investigation after being informed that Hodge was suspected of filing a false injury claim.

item.title: Gallagher Re confirms Minesh Jani’s appointment as India CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: How evolving relative valuations and diversifiers are redefining cat bond portfolios: Icosa Investments
item.description: With the first half of 2026 another remarkable period for catastrophe bond issuance, but the market remaining heavily concentrated on North American peak risks, a recent report by Icosa Investments AG has highlighted how diversifiers and normalised valuations between industry-loss-linked and indemnity structures is opening up additional relative-value opportunities. In the report, Steiger observes that beyond pure market size, the relative-value picture within the cat bond market has also evolved further in 2026. “Particularly striking is the improved relative valuation of so-called diversifiers.

item.title: Florida Citizens renews $2.82bn of reinsurance & cat bonds. Cites 30% YoY price decline
item.description: Over the entire risk transfer tower of almost $2.82 billion, which includes its still in-force cat bonds from prior years, Florida Citizens said the total program cost if approximately 20% lower than it was to place its risk transfer and reinsurance in 2025. Florida’s Citizens Property Insurance Corporation has now finalised its reinsurance risk transfer tower of almost $2.82 billion for 2026, securing $691 million of traditional reinsurance at the June 1 renewals, to add to its $2.125 billion of outstanding catastrophe bond protection. Notably, Florida Citizens said that new coverage placed in 2026 has come with a price that is roughly 30% lower than it would have cost for similar coverage a year ago.

item.title: Xceedance names Vikas Wadhera SVP to support growth of catastrophe risk services
item.description: Xceedance, a provider of technology-enabled services and operational solutions for the global insurance industry, has appointed Vikas Wadhera as Senior Vice President as the company seeks to further develop its catastrophe modelling and exposure management capabilities. Wadhera joins Xceedance with more than 27 years of experience in catastrophe modelling, actuarial analysis and risk analytics. He will focus on transforming legacy processes into scalable, modern workflows, enhancing user experience, and deploying AI-led solutions for intelligent data ingestion and analysis.” Wadhera added: “I am excited to join Xceedance at a time when insurers are rethinking how they manage catastrophe risk and exposure data, while accelerating digital transformation and leveraging AI to enhance underwriting, improve risk assessment, and enable faster, data-driven decision-making that ultimately delivers better outcomes for businesses.

item.title: Wiget to lead Swiss Re’s new Alternative Capital Solutions team
item.description: Launching on July 1st, the Alternative Capital Solutions team will be led by long-standing senior employee Mirjam Wiget. Global reinsurer Swiss Re has announced the launch of a new business unit, Alternative Capital Solutions, a division that will encompass its retrocession buying activities, as well as its insurance-linked securities (ILS). Wiget will be reporting to Martin Zingg, who has recently been appointed as the head of Swiss Re’s Alternative Capital Partners (ACP) division.

item.title: Flexpoint investment in Novel to support continued expansion
item.description: Novel Financial Holdings has secured an investment from Flexpoint Ford, a specialist private equity firm, to support its continued expansion as a diversified underwriting and insurance services holding company. Funded through Flexpoint Asset Opportunity Fund III and Flexpoint Insurance Opportunity Fund I, the capital investment broadens Novel’s ownership base and reinforces its position as an independent insurance platform. “As we continue to scale and diversify Novel, we are excited to partner with Flexpoint given their deep investment experience across the insurance value chain.

item.title: People Moves: Eason Hired by WTW to Strengthen Actuarial Data Science Capabilities
item.description: Eason brings nine years of insurance carrier experience, including strong expertise in actuarial and data science, predictive modelling and machine learning. WTW announced the appointment of Lizzie Eason as director and actuarial data scientist within its pricing, product, claims and underwriting (PPCU) consulting team in North America, effective June 15, 2026. Prior to that, Eason was a data science consultant at Nationwide.

item.title: Inszone Still on a Tear With Acquisition of Georgia Agency
item.description: Inszone Insurance Services, based in Sacramento, California, has continued its tear of mergers this year, most recently with the acquisition of Sanford, Bruker & Banks agency in Georgia. Inszone is owned by its leadership team and two private equity firms, Nationwide, the number of agency mergers and acquisitions was down in the first quarter of this year by 6%, compared with the same period last year, but that trend appears to be bottoming out, OPTIS Partners said in a recent report. The current owner is Jeffrey Banks, who, at age 73, said he looked to an Inszone merger to ensure the business would continue to thrive.

item.title: Tanker Traffic Through Hormuz Picks Up After Slower Flows Due to Crossing Concerns
item.description: Oil and liquefied natural gas tankers sailed through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, in a sign of traffic slowly picking up after Iran said it had again closed the waterway over the weekend, shipping data showed. Iran lifted its effective blockade of Hormuz last week after agreeing to a 60-day ceasefire with the United States while talks for a final peace deal take place. “Strait of Hormuz traffic began increasing, with commercial vessels continuing to route south … via Omani territorial waters and via the northern Iranian-controlled route,” the U.S.

item.title: Belfius enters France with Leocare acquisition
item.description: The company described France—the second-largest insurance market in Europe—as a strategic growth opportunity and said the acquisition provides a foundation for building a “scalable European digital insurance model.” Leocare cofounder Christophe Dandois will remain CEO of the company. Belgium’s Belfius has agreed to acquire 100% of Insurlytech, the parent company of French insurtech Leocare , marking its first expansion outside Belgium. Once completed, Insurlytech and Leocare will become part of Belfius Insurance, while Leocare will continue operating under its existing brand and management team.

item.title: Cheche Group launches AI-powered intelligent underwriting solution
item.description: Chinese auto insurance technology platform Cheche Group has launched ABAO Agent, an AI-powered underwriting and renewal agent designed to automate customer outreach, needs identification, policy follow-up, and policy conversion. We will continue integrating AI across the full insurance lifecycle, from underwriting and pricing to claims, with the goal of driving structural cost reductions for our carrier partners and cementing Cheche’s leadership in intelligent risk management for NEV insurance.” – Lei Zhang, Founder, CEO and Chairman of Cheche Group. “ABAO Agent is the core engine of Cheche’s intelligent transformation.

item.title: Breeze launches AI quoting tool
item.description: Digital cargo insurance startup Breeze has launched Quote AI Autofill, a new AI tool designed to reduce manual data entry for freight forwarders and make coverage easier to source. Quote AI Autofill is designed to remove repetitive manual tasks, speed up quotes, and keep the user fully in control.” – Eyal Goldberg, Chief Executive Officer, Breeze. Users can paste text or upload files they already hold, including emails, bills of lading, commercial invoices, booking confirmations, and screenshots.

item.title: Un taxi, dos voces y una misma visión del riesgo
item.description: Nuestro reconocible taxi rosa, convertido en un espacio en movimiento donde explorar cómo están cambiando los riesgos y cómo responder a ellos. Madrid se convirtió recientemente en una de las paradas clave de la gira europea con la que Beazley celebra su 80 aniversario.

item.title: Siete líderes y una misma convicción: las personas siguen siendo el motor del cambio
item.description: ¿Qué hace falta para liderar en un momento marcado por la transformación tecnológica, la incertidumbre y la necesidad de atraer y desarrollar talento? ¿Cómo se construyen equipos capaces de innovar, adaptarse y crecer?

item.title: AI-Generated Ads Should Be Exempt From EU Transparency Rules, Retail Association Says
item.description: Eurocommerce, the European retail association whose members include Amazon, H&M, Inditex, and Ikea, is asking EU tech chief Henna Virkkunen to exempt AI-generated advertisements from the bloc’s new regulation requiring disclosure of AI use. The European Union AI Act, which enters into force on August 2, requires companies to clearly label where artificial intelligence has been used to generate or modify images, video or audio content “constituting a deep fake.” In a letter sent to Virkkunen on Thursday and seen by Reuters on Friday, the industry group’s director general Christel Delberghe said AI-generated ads not meant to mislead users should not be included under the definition of “deep fake.” The regulation should not include AI-generated ads “not intended to mislead users, for example, generating an image of a living room to showcase a sofa, or enhancing product visuals for presentation purposes,” the letter said. Retailers are already using AI widely to generate marketing images, with German online retailer Zalando saying the technology has allowed it to cut content production costs by 90%, while fast-fashion retailers H&M and Zara are using AI-generated clones of models.

item.title: IMA Latest to Sue Howden Over Alleged Employee Poaching
item.description: Each of these clients has since issued a [broker-of-record] letter, changing their broker from IMA to Howden,” alleged IMA in the suit. IMA furthermore alleged Howden continues to solicit IMA clients, sometimes using IMA’s name to get meetings, and using confidential client information provided by Hamilton. Related: MMA Sues Former Specialty Marine Head Over Move to Howden US Michael Parrish, Howden’s CEO, Americas is also being sued by Marsh .

item.title: AI Medical Record Review: The Defensibility Standard for 2026
item.description: This post walks through what AI medical record review actually means in 2026, why generic LLMs fall short in claims and litigation, and the five things carriers should require from a vendor before signing a BAA. View Series AI medical record review is software that ingests claims documents (medical records, billing files, IME reports, correspondence), sorts and deduplicates them, and returns a source-linked chronology with page-level citations. AI medical record review is what gets that file from raw PDF to decision-ready chronology before lunch.

item.title: Investors get real-time view of UK bond market activity for the first time
item.description: For the first time, investors and market participants can access a single, real-time source of prices and trading activity across the UK bond market, following the launch of its bond consolidated tape, operated by ETS Connect UK. Today's launch of a consolidated tape gives investors a clear, reliable and comprehensive view of UK bond trading for the first time. Until now, data on bond trades was scattered across multiple sources, making it difficult to get a clear and complete picture of market activity.

item.title: FCA consults on proposals to support strong, consistent standards in the SIPP market
item.description: The proposals will bring greater certainty to the industry, improve confidence in the SIPP market and help ensure consumers can invest through SIPPs with greater confidence. Many firms are doing the right thing, but we want to help consumers invest with greater confidence by ensuring standards are consistent.' The FCA has set out plans to drive greater consistency of standards in self-invested pensions (SIPPs), while maintaining the flexibility and broad investment choice they offer.

item.title: Monevium Ltd enters special administration
item.description: On 18 June 2026, Monevium Ltd (Monevium) entered special administration. If you have any questions about the administration process, please contact the special administrators: As the firm continues to be authorised by us, we will engage with the special administrators and seek the best outcome for customers. The Payment and Electronic Money Institution Insolvency Regulations 2021 introduced a special administration regime for payment and e-money institutions.

item.title: European Investor Seedcamp Closes On $320M Across Two Funds To Back Seed Startups And Reaches $1B AUM
item.description: Seedcamp , one of Europe’s earliest seed investors, has closed on its 7th fund of $220 million and a select fund 2 of $100 million to invest in winners from the core fund. Crunchbase News spoke with Carlos Espinal , the firm’s managing partner who joined Seedcamp in 2010 and Hilary Howe , who rejoined the firm in 2022 to head up the select fund and establish a New York presence. Seedcamp also invests in software and vertical AI, but is careful about what is compelling and unique.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Robotics Startups On Fire As Venture Funding Surges To Record Numbers In 2026
item.description: The impressive rise in funding reflects a marked shift in perception among venture investors about the robotics sector, which was traditionally considered an expensive, asset-heavy hardware gamble. Robotics startup funding hit a record high in 2025, per Crunchbase data . And that trend is continuing in 2026 so far, with funding to the sector already eclipsing 2025’s totals.

item.title: Saas Isn’t Coming Back. Something Much Bigger Is Replacing It
item.description: But now, due to AI, where AI agents are replacing humans as the user (through what the industry calls “headless” models) and upending the per-seat model, the SaaS market has lost its predictability. That’s because AI-native software is going after a much larger opportunity than SaaS ever claimed and the productivity gains and value creation opportunities are unprecedented. It used to be that if you invested in SaaS, you slept well at night.

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending June 21st 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending June 21st 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending June 21st 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: Swiss Re renames ILS investment management unit Swiss Re Insurance-Linked Strategies Inc.
item.description: It makes sense to simplify the name, now all ILS investment strategies and funds fall under the remit of a single business unit, now Swiss Re Insurance-Linked Strategies Inc., for their management. is the new name for the global reinsurance firm’s dedicated insurance-linked securities investment management business, as Swiss Re looks to provide a clearer representation of the value proposition of this unit. For the full-history of the ILS management business unit names, you have to go back to 2020 when Swiss Re first established Swiss Re Insurance-Linked Investment Management Ltd.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Re & Safehub support parametric earthquake & volcanic insurance for Mexico
item.description: Liberty Mutual Reinsurance (LM Re), the reinsurance arm of the insurance group, and Safehub, a provider of seismic sensor and risk management technology, have partnered to provide the Mexican federal government with a new nationwide parametric earthquake and volcanic risk insurance solution. The solution is supported by ShakeNet Parametric, LM Re’s earthquake parametric and risk management technology solution, triggered by Safehub’s ShakeNet Parametric, and powered by the Global Earthquake Model’s (GEM) OpenQuake engine. Both Liberty Mutual Re and Safehub are renowned leaders in this space.

item.title: Mexico partners with LM Re for new parametric earthquake and volcanic insurance program
item.description: The Mexican federal government, through its sovereign disaster risk financing strategy, has deployed a nationwide parametric earthquake and volcanic risk insurance with Liberty Mutual Reinsurance (LM Re), part of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, acting as the lead capacity provider. “This insurance also represents the largest ShakeNet Parametric deployment to date, reflecting growing demand for high-resolution earthquake impact data in risk transfer applications, with additional large-scale projects currently being developed in markets around the world.” Jean-Christophe Garaix, head of parametrics and agriculture at LM Re, commented, “We are delighted to announce our partnership with the Mexican government to support their parametric earthquake program which demonstrates the strength of our parametric solutions.” Andy Thompson, CEO at Safehub, added, “This solution demonstrates how increased seismic monitoring can strengthen disaster resilience at a national scale. This parametric insurance solution, brokered by Augment Risk and domestic broker TBS, reportedly complements Mexico’s long-standing catastrophe risk financing strategy, forming part of a broader risk transfer framework aimed at ensuring fiscal resilience and continuity of public services after disasters.

item.title: Howden Re establishes Ireland office, David Carpenter to lead as Director
item.description: David Carpenter has been appointed Director, Howden Re Ireland. Howden Re, the reinsurance arm of broking group Howden, has expanded and strengthened its European presence by establishing a new office in Ireland. Together, we will bring clients closer to the insight, capacity and capital they need, delivered by a team that understands the market and its direction.” Matthew Westerman, Managing Director, Howden Re International, UK&I, said, “Ireland is an important market for our clients, particularly across the domestic insurance space, but also for global clients with complementary Irish portfolios, as well as the MGA and captive segments where demand for specialist reinsurance continues to grow.

item.title: Earnix launches AI orchestration system to improve insurers’ decision-making
item.description: Earnix, a Dynamic-AI platform for insurance, has announced the launch of AIOS, the AI Orchestration System for Insurance, bringing intelligence, agents, models, workflows, governance, and human oversight into a single decisioning engine designed to improve insurer performance. AIOS extends Earnix’s intelligent decisioning capabilities across the insurance lifecycle, enabling insurers to apply AI where it can have the greatest impact on business outcomes. Robin Gilthorpe, Chief Executive Officer of Earnix, said, “Insurers are entering a new phase of AI, where value will be measured by business performance rather than experimentation.

item.title: Hormuz Transit Security Is ‘Hour to Hour’ Play, Chubb CEO Says
item.description: US efforts to open shipping channels in the Strait of Hormuz will allow a gradual increase in vessel transit, though security remains volatile, Chubb Ltd. and Chubb announced on Friday a joint $400 million marine war risk consortium offering companies insurance for passage through the Hormuz strait. Read more: Markets/Coverages: Chubb Leads New Lloyd’s War Risk Consortium for Hormuz Shipping The US International Development Finance Corp.

item.title: After the Fracture: How Britain’s Financial Industry Recovered From Brexit
item.description: Other signs too suggest the British financial industry has weathered Brexit better than many expected: employment in the City of London financial district is near an all-time high, and banks are posting record profits. Read more: New UK Referendum Would Flip Brexit Result of a Decade Ago, Poll Finds But interviews with executives and data reviewed by Reuters paint a more nuanced picture, of Britain as a financial center whose dominance has been eroded while the country itself has become less attractive for some investors. bank could shift 4,000 jobs from Britain, joining a chorus of executives who warned a vote to leave the European Union would ravage the country’s finance industry.

item.title: KPMG Australia Scandal Widens After it Confirms Optus Data Was Misused
item.description: In the second major scandal to hit a Big Four accounting firm in Australia since 2023, KPMG has been under fire after the whistleblower alleged it misused confidential board papers from real estate company Lendlease to support bids for major audit tenders for Westpac, a large bank, and Dexus, a property firm. KPMG Australia staff shared sensitive information about telecom firm Optus with another internal team bidding for an audit contract for its rival Telstra in a breach of ethics, the firm’s chairman told a parliamentary hearing on Friday. KPMG Australia’s former CEO, Andrew Yates, said that the Optus-related evidence, which emerged from an investigation by law firm Allens, had prompted his resignation last month.

item.title: Asia aviation insurers continue to push for rate hikes as historic claims, Russia exposure linger
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Matthew Davis joins Delta Insurance Australia as head of claims
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: GT Insurance taps Yanna Walters as chief strategy, innovation officer
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Week in Review — June 15–18, 2026
item.description: IAG invested in mental-health platform Sonder; Securian Canada backed June Health. The WSJ reported on State Farm’s agency overhaul , noting that agents who want to remain with the carrier beyond 2027 will need to sign new contracts with revised compensation structures and sales targets. The changes appear to be a defensive response to rising distribution costs, the emergence of AI-driven direct sales, and Progressive’s growing momentum.

item.title: dodoAI raises ¥280M seed for enterprise AI governance platform
item.description: Tokyo-based dodoAI has closed a ¥280 million seed round led by Genesia Ventures. dodoAI offers what it calls a “Sovereign Agentic OS,” a governance layer for AI agents performing corporate work. The company disclosed one insurance use case: a pilot project with a large property and casualty insurer in which dodoAI applied AI-driven development to a single subsystem within a core platform consisting of roughly 300 subsystems.

item.title: Generali launches the Generali Excellence Academy
item.description: Generali has launched the Generali Excellence Academy, a Group-wide learning and upskilling program for its roughly 90,000 employees. The Generali Excellence Academy will play a key role in fostering a culture where human talent and technology work together, enabling our organisation to adapt, innovate and remain close to the customers. The academy is headquartered in Palazzo Berlam in Trieste and is positioned as an enabler of the carrier’s “Lifetime Partner 27: Driving Excellence” three-year plan.

item.title: Guardian expands employee vision benefits with XP Health
item.description: Guardian has partnered with digital vision benefits provider XP Health to add a new vision care option to its employee benefits ecosystem. The collaboration gives employers access to XP Health’s digital-first vision platform, which includes virtual and at-home frame try-ons, online prescription renewals, and scheduling tools for routine eye exams. The partnership expands Guardian’s portfolio of digital well-being offerings, which also includes family-building support through Carrot, caregiving support through Wellthy, cancer support services through Osara, and wellness content from Peloton.

item.title: Rival launches RPM Quoting for insurance brokers
item.description: Sharp Insurance, one of Rival’s customers, said the addition of quoting within RPM allows brokers to move from opportunity management to quote generation without leaving the platform, creating a more streamlined workflow. Rival Insurance Technology has launched RPM Quoting, a new rating and quoting capability embedded within its Rival Platform & Marketplace (RPM), as part of the company’s June Gemini release. Based in Toronto, Rival develops technology solutions for insurance brokers, MGAs, and industry partners through products including RPM, Reveal, and other connected insurance technology offerings.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Banco de Bogotá compra banca minorista de Itaú, y Bitso habilita corredor a Asia
item.description: Bancolombia disponibiliza dólares digitales a través de Wenia El banco permitirá a sus clientes colombianos comprar y vender dólares digitales a través de la plataforma criptoexchange Wenia, perteneciente al mismo Grupo Cibest. Wenia habilita pagos con euros digitales a través de su tarjeta La plataforma incorporó el uso de euros digitales (EURC) en su tarjeta Wenia Card, permitiendo realizar pagos en comercios físicos y en línea que acepten Mastercard. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: Celent Model Insurer Winners Show Innovation’s Operational Turn
item.description: That theme was evident across Celent’s Model Insurer winners, which included Prudential Financial (Newark, N.J.) as Model Insurer of the Year, along with insurers recognized for customer experience, data, analytics and AI, innovation execution, and legacy and ecosystem transformation. Taken together, Celent’s 2026 Model Insurer winners suggest that the industry’s innovation agenda is becoming more disciplined and operationally grounded. Credit: Ajay Suresh/Wikipedia.) Celent ’s (Boston) 2026 annual Model Insurer Awards point to a maturing phase of insurance innovation—one in which AI, cloud modernization and digital experience initiatives are increasingly judged by their ability to change operating models rather than by novelty alone.

item.title: Flood Insurance Gap Will Squeeze Local Governments and Homeowners, Moody’s Says
item.description: Some of those costs are shifted onto impacted households that do not carry adequate (or any) flood insurance, onto federal assistance programs, and—more and more—to state and local governments, Saleh said. At the same time, participation in the National Flood Insurance Program has declined significantly in recent years, leaving some homeowners more exposed than ever, the Moody’s team pointed out. And the consequences of that gap are about to worsen for property owners and state and local governments, thanks to increasing rainfall amounts, stronger storm surge, more development in flood zones, cutbacks in federal aid, and aging river levees around the country.

item.title: Trump Says Illegal Immigration Increased Car Insurance Prices
item.description: President Donald Trump this week credited his tough immigration policies for a drop in car insurance premiums, falsely blaming illegal immigration during his predecessor’s time in the White House for a prior increase. CLAIM: Trump’s tough immigration policies have led to a drop in car insurance premiums, after a spike under the Biden administration caused by illegal immigration. “After over a year of ZERO ILLEGAL IMMIGRATION, and our highly successful efforts to REVERSE the Biden Invasion, Car Insurance Premiums have come tumbling down.” But experts say that although the data in the graphic aligns with industry trends, it was chiefly the COVID-19 pandemic — not illegal immigration — that caused the uptick.

item.title: Data Centers Are Being Built in Areas Exposed to Extreme Weather
item.description: A significant number of data centers are being built in areas that are exposed to extreme weather risks brought on by climate change, a report by XDI has found. More than 70 of the roughly 150 high-risk data centers are set to be built in North America, compared with 45 in Europe and 12 in East Asia, the analysis found. “A data center designed to remain operational during an extreme weather event may still fail if surrounding infrastructure is not,” it said.
